sourceFiles.name: thermal-system

fix.title: Revise cooling fan threshold to activate before emergency shutdown
fix.description: This fix removes the redundancy by making the cooling fan activation operationally reachable before emergency shutdown. The fan activation requirement text and supporting threshold attribute are revised from 85°C to 75°C, preserving both intents in a consistent sequence: mitigation first, shutdown second.

changes.description: Revised the cooling fan activation requirement threshold from 85°C to 75°C so the fan can activate before emergency shutdown at 80°C, eliminating the unreachable redundant condition.
Updated the supporting CoolingSystem attribute to match the revised reachable cooling fan activation threshold.

results.name: 04_redundancy
results.rawText:
System characterisation:
- System purpose: A thermal control/shutdown model fragment defining when a system should shut down and when a cooling fan should activate.
- Operational domain: Thermal management / industrial or embedded control.
- Model scope: Small model fragment containing two requirement definitions and one part definition.
- Known scope limitations: No behaviour model, state machine, verification cases, decomposition hierarchy, interfaces, allocations, or package structure beyond a single package are present. Checks depending on those elements are assessed as not evidenced in this fragment and skipped where appropriate.

ISSUE [REDUNDANCY-001]
Element(s):     thermal_system::EmergencyShutdown; thermal_system::CoolingFanActivation; thermal_system::CoolingSystem::emergencyShutdownThresholdC; thermal_system::CoolingSystem::coolingFanStartThresholdC
Check:          8. States that are never activated
Finding:        The model defines a cooling fan activation condition at 85°C while also defining emergency shutdown at 80°C. If shutdown prevents further operation, the fan activation condition is unreachable and therefore redundant in the model context provided.
Evidence:       Requirement text: "The system shall enter emergency shutdown when temperature reaches 80 degrees Celsius." Requirement text: "The cooling fan shall turn on when temperature reaches 85 degrees Celsius." Supporting attributes: "attribute emergencyShutdownThresholdC : Real = 80.0;" and "attribute coolingFanStartThresholdC : Real = 85.0;"
Score:          0.4  -  Significant defect because one trigger likely makes the other unreachable, creating redundant intent and likely incorrect behavioural interpretation.
Confidence:     MEDIUM  -  The threshold conflict is explicit, but certainty is limited because no state machine or operational semantics are provided showing whether shutdown absolutely precludes fan activation.
Risk:           Reviewers may assume the cooling function provides mitigation when in practice the system would already have shut down first, leading to inconsistent requirements interpretation, wasted design effort, or invalid verification planning.
Recommendation: Remove or revise one of the thresholds so the two functions are operationally consistent, or explicitly model/annotate the intended sequence (e.g., fan starts before shutdown, or fan may still run during shutdown) with behaviour and rationale.

CHECK PASSED: 1. Duplicate Requirements  -  The two requirements were examined for identical or near-identical wording and no duplicated requirement text or copied hierarchy/package restatement was present.

CHECK PASSED: 2. Duplicate Block or Part Definitions  -  The fragment contains only one part definition and no parallel block, item, or interface definitions that duplicate the same concept.

CHECK PASSED: 3. Redundant Relationships  -  No satisfy, allocate, connect, bind, or specialization relationships are present, so no duplicated relationships were found in this fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 4. Redundant Behaviour Models  -  No state machines, action definitions, sequences, or message exchange models are present, so behavioural duplication is not evidenced.

CHECK PASSED: 5. Redundant Attributes & Constraints  -  The attributes in CoolingSystem are distinct threshold/temperature values and do not duplicate the same attribute or derived value across levels.

CHECK PASSED: 6. Redundant Decomposition  -  No decomposition hierarchy, intermediate pass-through blocks, empty packages, or action decompositions are present in this model fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 7. Unintentional vs. Intentional Redundancy  -  No structural or requirement duplication suggesting intentional architectural redundancy was found; the only concern is the potentially unreachable fan activation condition captured in ISSUE [REDUNDANCY-001].

Summary Table

| ID | Score | Confidence | One-line description |
|---|---:|---|---|
| REDUNDANCY-001 | 0.4 | MEDIUM | Cooling fan activation at 85°C is likely redundant because emergency shutdown occurs earlier at 80°C. |

Overall Assessment Score: 0.4
Model Status for this Dimension: Critical deficiencies - major rework required before this model can be used
